mechanism: candor-ts classified Env ONLY for a direct process.env.KEY dot access; it silently missed process.env["KEY"] (bracket), const env=process.env; env.KEY (alias), const {KEY}=process.env (destructure), and "KEY" in process.env (membership).
evidence: Dogfooding chalk/supports-color found env reads reported 0 Env (silent, engine-specific); fixed 2026-07-15 (extend the recognizer to bracket/alias/destructure/in); no fabrication; gated.

mechanism: A use path::X inside a NESTED BLOCK ({ use std::process::Command; Command::new(..).status(); } / an if/else arm / match arm / loop body) was not tracked -> the call resolved to nothing -> PURE (module-level and fn-body-level uses worked; only block-nested were missed).

mechanism: The staged 0.16 callgraph-aware baseline guard's exit-1 (CI-breaking) fired on an Unknown-ONLY gain (a formerly-pure fn now reading pure->Unknown from syntactic dispatch-resolution variance or an unstable JVM anonymous-class $N name), even though Unknown is the trust marker not a real capability gain.
evidence: Real-world corpus test (10 rust crate pairs + gson) found 3 rust + 1 JVM fires, ALL gained-Unknown, ZERO a real capability gain (serde byte-identical source flipped pure->Unknown; gson $9 anon-class name unstable) -- a false-alarm class that would break CI on innocuous bumps; RESOLVED 2026-07-16 four-way (ratchet only on a real boundary effect; Unknown-only = advisory exit 0); PART 15c.
